title: The Magic of Mooska-Kadooska
summary: In the deep, winding tunnels of a mountain home, a young student named Seeker prepares for his first lesson in the magical arts. Guided by the wise mole teacher, Ove, Seeker discovers that spells are powered by imagination and rhyme. When the class begins practicing disappearance and reappearance magic, Seeker invents his own unique incantation, 'Mooska-Kadooska,' setting off a journey of wonder in the darkness of the subterranean school.

highlightQuote.text: Keep telling the story and then you'll find out.

transcript.text: Do you want to do a Young Seeker story, when he's a kiddo? High in the mountains, in his family's tunnels?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Mhm.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Maybe when he's learning about magic?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Mhm.

Once upon a time, Young Seeker woke up and realized that it was almost time for school to start. And it was a big day at school because they were going to learn about casting magic spells. And he was very excited to learn magic spells, as he liked learning about stone carving and architecture, which is about designing the shapes of buildings. But he was especially excited to learn about magic. And so he got up and got dressed, and ate some breakfast, and went to class. And when he arrived, they were just about to begin. And who was the teacher?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: The teacher was the mole named Ove.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A mole named Ove. And Ove was a very wise old mole who had been doing magic for many years. And he said, 'Welcome class. Today we will learn the very first parts of the magical arts. Now class, do any of you know how to cast a magic spell already?' And did anyone raise their hand?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No.

No, nobody raised their hand because they didn't know any magic spells. And Seeker thought to himself, 'Oh my gosh, I'm excited.' What kind of magic spell did he want to learn how to cast? Oh, your mouth is full of pancakes, haha.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Keep telling the story and then you'll find out.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: So Seeker thought to himself, 'I hope that we can learn some magic spells that help us magically make things out of rocks, or magically make light appear, or magically make fire, or magically make me able to fly through the air.'
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Or to make flower gardens.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Or to make flower gardens, oh.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Or to be superheroes.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: A superhero spell, yes.
Which one of those do you pick?
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Well, Seeker's head was full of ideas, but he listened to the teacher, and the teacher Ove said, 'Yes everyone, you're probably wondering how you can cast a spell to make things explode, boom! Or to do lots of mischief, but...'
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Well, well, well, mischief can only do mischief because of bad guys.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. And so the teacher said, 'We will start small. Hmm. Let's learn about a spell to make things disappear and reappear. And so, as you know already, little ones...'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, clean up that thing. There's a red thing in there.
A red thing? What do you think it is?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: A piece of blueberry.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I see. Can I get it out with your fork?
Mhm. I'm more pancakes please.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh. Shall I make you some pieces of this one? Okay, here you go. Piece. There you go. And that's, I think that's all that's left. So as you know, said Ove, magic spells must rhyme. But each time you cast one, you can make up a new rhyme if you like.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Seeker said, 'And if you want to make a new rhyme, I have one in mind that I'd like to use: mooska-kadooska,' said Seeker.
Mooska-kadooska. And teacher Ove said, 'Yes, that's an excellent rhyme.' And then after the rhyme, you just say the magic word for the spell you want to cast. So here...
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Dad, get that pancake! Oops, a little pancake.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Actually, if you hold, whoa, oh no, I dropped it. And if you hold still please, I will also get the syrup on your tower because I don't want you to get sticky feet. Actually, if you keep holding still, I will wipe it with some water to make it not sticky anymore. All right, it'll just be a tiny bit wet. Okay, you can put your feet down now. And so teacher Ove said, 'Watch this.' And he held up, what little object did he hold up to make disappear? Was it an apple?
A dice.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: He held up dice, and he said, 'Mooska-kadooska, disappear!' And then there was a little floop, and his hand was empty. And he said, 'Look at that.' And he waved it around, and the dice were gone. And he said, 'Does anyone want to guess what we can do next?'
They'll reappear.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Well, we can make the dice come back with the same magic word but the word reappear after. So ready? Mooska-kadooska, reappear! Sloop! And the dice popped back into existence on his hand.
More piece of pancake.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: I couldn't understand what you're saying because your mouth is full of pancake. You better chew and swallow. Oh, you spit it out a little bit, yuck. Let's just take things slow here.
No, Dad, clean up my...
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh, oh please don't shake it like that, you flung pancake everywhere. Oh my goodness.
Wow. That's wet.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, because your water bottle tipped over and a little bit poured out of it because the nozzle was popped open.
Dad, clean my water bottle.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: I will, I will wash it when I wash your breakfast things. Would you like a napkin?
Dad, I'm all sticky. I need a bath.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: I think we can get you un-sticky, but I think probably the first step is to take off your sticky shirt. Can you do that please? One sleeve off, another sleeve off, and then... bloop. All right.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Oh, Dad, now I need a wet paper towel.
Yes, coming right up. Let's get you un-sticky.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: And also soap.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Well, are you going to finish your bacon?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: I am.
Okay, let's just do that first and then we'll get you cleaned up.
transcript.speaker: Dad

characters.name: Seeker
characters.description: A brave dwarven explorer and mediator, he resides in a glowing castle hub—a multiversal dock for skyships anchored within the skyline of New York City. From this urban sanctum, he conducts silver slime experiments and tends to wandering cats amidst a massive library. Frequently swapping between a red coat and a mystical purple cloak, his braided red beard remains his most eccentric tool, capable of catching small fish. A master of fusion, he combines ancient stone-craft with high-velocity mechanics, utilizing a legendary magic ladder to scale the shifting architecture of the realms. Whether brewing portal potions, harvesting floof-berries in Limbo with gemstone goggles, or aiding Buddy the Mouse, this walnut-loving student of the stars remains the hero who orchestrated the daring rescue of the world.

characters.name: Ove
characters.description: A wise old mole and magic teacher who instructs students on how to use rhyming spells to manipulate objects.

places.name: The Mountain Tunnels
places.description: The subterranean home and school where Young Seeker and his family live high in the mountains.
